codeReview.summary: I reviewed the two prior failed reviews, the round-1 synthesis, the fix feature metadata, the worker handoff/transcript skeleton, and commit `fd51c2d` against the current repository state. The fix closes the two scoped blocking gaps: enum constructor inference now rejects conflicting repeated type-parameter bindings in both tuple and struct variant inference paths (`lib/typecheck.ml:757`, `lib/typecheck.ml:813`, `lib/typecheck.ml:1285`), and codegen now recursively unifies nested payload AST types when inferring generic enum constructor instantiations (`lib/codegen.ml:406`, `lib/codegen.ml:486`, `lib/codegen.ml:1342`). The accompanying coverage in `test/test_typecheck.ml:2430-2435`, `test/test_typecheck.ml:2498-2500`, `test/test_codegen_ownership.ml:1129-1160`, and `test/test_codegen_ownership.ml:1201-1220`, plus the generated fixture at `.factory/runtime/generic-enum-nested.go:74-85,111`, provide evidence that the original blocking findings are resolved for the intended direct generic-enum ownership flows.

summary: Pass. This fix adequately resolves the prior blocking findings it targeted: repeated generic enum type-parameter bindings now fail on conflicts, and nested payload constructor instantiations are preserved through Go generation for the covered direct generic-enum ownership paths.
